checks.evidence: The rendered docs overview describes 'AI-powered web scraper' and 'data extraction', and the pricing page says 'visual web scraper to extract data from websites'. Consistent with 'Web Scraping / Data Extraction'.

The authentication docs page explicitly states 'The MrScraper API uses API token authentication' via the 'x-api-token' header, with analytics endpoints using query-parameter auth. Matches pass-1's ['api_key'] claim.
Pricing page shows 'Scraper Free $0/month' with API tokens. Billing docs state 'Free plan includes 100 tokens per month with no credit card required.' Self-serve free access confirmed.

API Overview docs confirm REST API at api.app.mrscraper.com with JSON, authentication, pagination, error handling. Sidebar shows extensive endpoints: V3 Platform, Marketplace (Shopee, Tiktok, Lazada, etc.), Travel (Agoda, Booking.com, etc.), PDP Cache, Analytics. Python SDK, Webhooks, and CLI documented.

Rendered docs sidebar on multiple pages (overview, billing) lists 'MCP Server' as a navigation item under 'Getting Started'. This directly contradicts pass-1's claim that no official MCP server was found ('none_found').

All core evidence for buildability is intact: public REST API, API-key auth, free tier (no CC required), Python SDK, webhooks, extensive docs. The presence of MCP docs actually strengthens the verdict.

corrections.reason: Pass-1 claimed no official MCP server was found, but the rendered docs sidebar clearly includes 'MCP Server' as a navigation item under 'Getting Started' on multiple pages (overview, billing). This indicates that official MCP documentation exists, contradicting the 'none_found' claim.
corrections.evidence: The sidebar of https://docs.mrscraper.com/docs/getting-started/overview (and billing page) lists: Overview, Quickstart, Playground, Token Plan, CLI, MCP Server, Migrating from Platform v2 to v3, Billing. The presence of a dedicated 'MCP Server' page in the official docs contradicts pass-1's assertion that no official MCP server exists.
